title: Telco Customer Churn Prediction
description: End-to-end ML platform predicting customer churn using XGBoost with hyperparameter tuning via Optuna, tracked with MLflow. Deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with a Gradio UI for real-time predictions.
longDescription: This project builds a complete churn prediction pipeline: preprocessing, feature engineering, XGBoost model training, hyperparameter optimization with Optuna, and experiment tracking via MLflow. Real-time predictions are accessible through a REST API (FastAPI) and an interactive Gradio UI, deployed securely on AWS ECS Fargate.
problem: High customer churn leads to revenue loss, and companies often lack proactive predictive tools.
solution: Implemented a scalable ML pipeline, optimized model performance with Optuna, tracked experiments with MLflow, and provided an intuitive web interface for real-time predictions.
results: Enabled actionable insights for retention strategies and demonstrated expertise in ML modeling, cloud deployment, and end-to-end pipeline design.

title: Semantic Book Recommender – Hugging Face Deployment
description: Developed an intelligent book recommendation system using Python, LangChain, and Hugging Face Transformers. Implemented vector search with OpenAI embeddings for semantic similarity.
longDescription: Built a semantic recommendation engine for books that goes beyond keywords, using embeddings to capture content meaning. Implemented vector search with FAISS and deployed the system on Hugging Face Spaces for interactive use.
problem: Traditional recommendation systems fail to capture deeper semantic relationships, offering less relevant suggestions.
solution: Leveraged Hugging Face Transformers, OpenAI embeddings, and LangChain to create a vector-based search engine that understands meaning, not just keywords.
results: Delivered highly accurate book recommendations, providing users with contextually relevant suggestions through an interactive deployment.

title: Trash Detection – Capstone Project
description: Developed a real-time trash detection system for mobile robots using YOLOv11n with transfer learning, deployed in outdoor environments for urban sustainability initiatives.
longDescription: This capstone project delivers a computer vision solution for autonomous trash detection. Leveraging YOLOv11n, the system was trained on the TACO dataset and custom street-level images. Extensive data annotation, augmentation, and hyperparameter tuning improved model generalization and robustness under varied lighting and environmental conditions. The optimized inference pipeline was embedded for low-latency deployment on robotic platforms, supporting real-time detection and tracking.
problem: Urban litter negatively impacts communities and current robotic detection systems often struggle with accuracy and real-time deployment in varied outdoor environments.
solution: Applied transfer learning on YOLOv11n, engineered a high-quality annotated dataset, implemented targeted augmentation, and optimized the model for embedded deployment with Python and OpenCV. Collaborated with robotics engineers to integrate the system into autonomous navigation pipelines.
results: Achieved mAP@0.5: 82%, mAP@0.5–0.95: 0.51, precision: 0.85, and recall: 0.71. Reduced inference latency by 40%, improved real-time tracking stability by 30%, and demonstrated a scalable solution for urban trash detection.

title: RAG Resume Assistant
description: Implemented a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline with LangChain, FAISS, and OpenAI to create an AI assistant capable of answering recruiter queries on resumes.
longDescription: This AI-powered assistant enables recruiters to query resumes conversationally. It indexes resumes with FAISS for semantic retrieval and uses OpenAI models to generate precise answers to recruiter-style questions.
problem: Recruiters spend significant time manually scanning resumes, slowing down the hiring process.
solution: Built a RAG pipeline that combines vector search and LLMs to answer queries efficiently and accurately, improving HR workflow.
results: Reduced screening time for recruiters and showcased practical application of AI in HR technology.

title: Traffic Congestion Prediction using Big Data
description: Processed 94.5M+ rows of NYC DOT traffic data using Apache Spark on Google Cloud Dataproc. Integrated ELK stack for monitoring and visualization.
longDescription: Built a scalable big data pipeline to process massive NYC traffic datasets, detect congestion trends, and visualize results. Utilized Apache Spark for distributed processing and the ELK stack for monitoring and dashboards.
problem: Traffic congestion in cities causes lost productivity and environmental issues, and large-scale datasets are challenging to process.
solution: Implemented a distributed processing pipeline on GCP using Spark and integrated ELK for real-time monitoring and visualization.
results: Processed 94.5M+ rows in under 10 minutes and provided actionable congestion insights via dashboards.

title: Leafio – Plant Disease Detection
description: Developed an Android app powered by a CNN model for real-time plant disease detection, served via Flask API with 85%+ accuracy.
longDescription: Leafio allows farmers to detect crop diseases using smartphone cameras. A custom CNN model predicts diseases with high accuracy, served via Flask API, and integrated into an Android frontend.
problem: Farmers often lack fast, reliable methods to detect crop diseases, affecting yield.
solution: Trained a CNN on thousands of leaf images, served predictions via Flask, and built an Android interface for real-time detection.
results: Achieved 85%+ accuracy and reduced disease identification time from days to seconds, improving farming efficiency.
